title: umbilicus vulgaris
intro: Umbilicus vulgaris, also known as common orvey or creeping Jenny, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the family Crassulaceae, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reducing swelling. The health benefits of Umbilicus vulgaris include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infections. The herbal preparations of Umbilicus vulgaris include teas, infusions, and poultices, often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
